heading: Everything you need to master algorithms
subtitle: Go beyond reading — interact with every step, inspect the code, and compare strategies side by side.

modules.title: Scrub through every algorithm state
modules.subtitle: Move forward and backward through each step at your own pace. Never lose track of where the algorithm is or why it made a decision.

items.label: Forward & backward scrubbing
items.description: Step through any point in the execution — revisit tricky states without restarting.

items.label: Auto-play with speed control
items.description: Watch it run automatically at 0.5× to 4× speed, or pause and inspect at any moment.

items.label: Active element highlighting
items.description: Every comparison, swap, and pointer change is visually highlighted in real time.

modules.title: Pseudocode in sync with every step
modules.subtitle: The code panel tracks exactly which line is executing as the visualization runs, bridging the gap between theory and implementation.

items.label: Line-by-line sync
items.description: The active pseudocode line highlights automatically as the visualizer advances.

items.label: Multiple language views
items.description: See the same algorithm expressed in pseudocode, Python, or JavaScript.

items.label: Step annotations
items.description: Inline notes explain why each line matters in the context of the current state.

modules.title: Run two algorithms at once
modules.subtitle: Load the same dataset into two visualizers and watch them race. Instantly see the performance and strategy differences between any two algorithms.

items.label: Synchronized playback
items.description: Both visualizers step in lockstep so comparisons stay meaningful.

items.label: Live Big-O stats
items.description: Operations count and time/space complexity shown for each algorithm in real time.

items.label: Custom input support
items.description: Test with your own array or graph data to see how each algorithm handles edge cases.
